name: Step 3.7 Flash
archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with 1.8B vision encoder + 196B language backbone, 198B total / 11B active
note: Newest StepFun release (successor to Step 3.5 Flash), adding native multimodal (image/GUI/document) support that the text-only Step 3.5 Flash lacked. Not previously in corpus (corpus's newest StepFun entry is Step-3 from 2025-07-31). No shipped 'Step 4' flagship found -- only reported as in training.

results.notes: Note: this is the Artificial Analysis composite Intelligence Index (30), not a GPQA-specific figure -- no single-benchmark independent score could be verified for this exact model. [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The existing entry mislabeled Artificial Analysis's composite Intelligence Index (30) as a 'gpqa' score -- the record's own verificationNotes already admit this was not a real GPQA figure. StepFun's official GitHub README states a real GPQA Diamond avg@16 item accuracy (over 3168 records) of 78.41% for the NVFP4 checkpoint (no MTP) vs 77.81% for the NVFP4+MTP speculative-decoding checkpoint. No pl]
SWE-Bench Verified score from StepFun's official Step 3.7 Flash launch-blog benchmark table (own column: Step 3.7 Flash 76.5%, vs. e.g. Claude Opus 4.7 87.6%, GPT 5.5 not tested).
Confirmed identically in both the official GitHub README ('secured a definitive second-place finish on SWE-Bench PRO with a score of 56.3') and the official launch blog's benchmark table.

Terminal-Bench 2.1 score from the official launch-blog benchmark table (59.6%); the GitHub README's narrative text and chart tooltip round this to 59.5% -- both cite the same StepFun-run evaluation, minor rounding variance only.

'It reaches 75.82% on BrowseComp, approaching larger models such as Claude Opus 4.7 and GLM 5.1' -- stated directly in StepFun's official launch blog text and confirmed in the accompanying benchmark chart (75.8%).

Text-only (no tool use) Humanity's Last Exam score, from the launch blog's benchmark table row 'HLE w. tool (acc) 47.2% (text-only 49.7%)'. The tool-augmented figure (47.2%) is a different, non-comparable setting and was not used.

Raw GDPval-AA points score (unit matches taxonomy's 'pts, range 0-2000') from the launch blog's benchmark table: 'GDPval-Stirrup 1415.8 (ii 45.8%)'. The normalized percentage form (45.8%) is what StepFun's GitHub README instead reports as 'GDPval-AA: 45.8' -- both numbers describe the same underlying evaluation, but 1415.8 is the correct value for this taxonomy slug's declared point-scale unit.

Not one of the 6 originally-flagged core gaps (mmmu-pro is not a core ranking slug), but surfaced during the mmmu investigation as a genuine, citable, correctly-slugged data point worth capturing rather than discarding.
Resolved via new source type #4 (other leaderboards/aggregators not in the first pass's checklist) -- specifically MathArena.ai, discovered during this round's sweep and not previously checked.

verificationNotes:
Artificial Analysis independently scored the Intelligence Index at 30 (#34/94); no independently-verified GPQA/LiveCodeBench numbers located for this specific model (only for the prior Step 3.5 Flash), so composite index is the only result reported.

2026-07-16 gap-fill pass on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval/livecodebench/mmmu: checked StepFun's own launch materials (GitHub README, HF model card, static.stepfun.com launch blog) and none report MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, or MMMU/MMMU-Pro for Step 3.7 Flash -- the launch table was deliberately restricted to a newer agentic/coding suite (ClawEval-1.1, Toolathlon, HLE w. Tool, SWE-Bench PRO, Terminal-Bench 2.1, GDPval-AA, BrowseComp, SimpleVQA, V*). Cross-checked independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis's current Intelligence Index v4.1 methodology (the version used for this model's page) explicitly enumerates its evaluation set as GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, and AA-LCR -- none of the five legacy benchmarks are in AA's current suite at all, and llm-stats.com/organizations/stepfun does not list a Step 3.7 Flash entry, and BenchLM.ai's category breakdown explicitly shows "Math: 0 benchmarks, Not measured" for this model. Sibling model Step 3.5 Flash does carry its own AIME 2025 score elsewhere in third-party trackers, but that figure belongs to a different model and cannot substitute for Step 3.7 Flash's own (unpublished) result. Treated as a legitimate saturated/deprioritized-benchmark absence, not a research gap, for all six core slugs. One partial exception: Artificial Analysis independently measured Step 3.7 Flash on the harder MMMU-Pro variant (75%) -- added under the correct 'mmmu-pro' slug since base MMMU itself remains genuinely unpublished for this model.

2026-07-17 second-pass gap-fill on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, deliberately targeting source types NOT checked in the 2026-07-16 pass (which already exhausted official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, and BenchLM's aggregate view). New sources checked this round:

(1) Stanford HELM -- fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/, /helm/classic/latest/, and the HELM Capabilities launch post. HELM's evaluated-model roster is a small curated list of major-lab flagship models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.); no StepFun model of any generation appears on any HELM leaderboard, so this model has never been in HELM's independent-MMLU scope. Ruled out.

(2) arXiv/academic papers -- searched arxiv.org/Semantic-Scholar-style queries for "<model> MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval" baselines. The only Step-family arXiv paper found, 2602.10604 (Step 3.5 Flash technical report, v2 dated 2026-02-23), predates Step 3.7 Flash's 2026-05-29 release and does not mention it. No other paper was found citing Step 3.7 Flash as a baseline for any of the four benchmarks. Ruled out.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs -- searched for lm-evaluation-harness results.json or comparable committed run logs against stepfun-ai/Step-3.7-Flash; none found beyond the official repo README already reviewed in pass one. Ruled out.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- OpenRouter's model page is pricing/speed only, no benchmark scores. LiveCodeBench's leaderboard is a client-rendered JS app that did not surface readable StepFun data via fetch (inconclusive, not a confirmed ruling-out). PapersWithCode is defunct (shut down by Meta, July 2025) and redirects to Hugging Face -- no usable SOTA table. Scale AI's SEAL leaderboard search surfaced no Step 3.7 Flash entry. BenchLM.ai's model page was rechecked at the per-category (not just aggregate) level: Math = "Not measured" (consistent with the first pass's finding), Knowledge (which would carry MMLU) = "Score pending" with no number, Coding shows only an aggregate category score (50.2, rank 61/122) with no HumanEval-specific breakout. However, MathArena.ai -- a genuinely new, independent competition-math benchmarking project not in the first pass's checklist -- DOES test this exact model via live StepFun API access and reports AIME 2026 = 95.00% (+/-3.90% CI). This resolved the 'aime' gap (added above). MathArena does not track a Hendrycks-MATH/MATH-500-style benchmark (its categories are AIME, HMMT, Apex, ArXivMath, BrokenArXiv only), so it could not resolve 'math'.

(5) Non-English sources -- checked Chinese community coverage (Sohu, Zhihu, DataLearnerAI, 302.AI's "benchmark lab" review). 302.AI's review was directly fetched and confirmed to use only a custom qualitative 10-point rubric across 4 self-designed task categories (logic/math, intuition, multimodal, programming) -- no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval numbers reported. DataLearnerAI and Sohu pages could not be reached (DNS resolution failures in this environment) and the Zhihu post returned HTTP 403, so none of their content could be independently verified. Separately, a WebSearch-tool-generated summary asserted specific figures (MMLU=82.3, HumanEval=85.1) for this model; repeated targeted searches for those exact numbers found no page actually containing them, indicating this was very likely a search-summarizer hallucination rather than a real citable source -- it was deliberately NOT added to the corpus. No Korean- or Japanese-language source with a Step-3.7-Flash-specific MMLU/MATH/HumanEval number was found either.

(6) Sibling models -- Step 3.5 Flash (the direct predecessor) does carry its own self-reported MMLU (85.8) and HumanEval (81.1) figures in third-party comparison tables, but per this file's pre-existing verificationNotes these belong to a different model and are correctly not substituted in for Step 3.7 Flash.

Net outcome: 'aime' resolved via MathArena (independent, new source). 'mmlu', 'math', and 'humaneval' remain genuinely unpublished for this specific model after a good-faith check of all 6 newly-specified source types (HELM, arXiv, GitHub, other aggregators, non-English community sources, and sibling-model tables) -- proposed as omitted_no_evidence, consistent with the prior pass's conclusion that StepFun deliberately restricted this model's public benchmark disclosure to a newer agentic/coding/search suite.
